meta.title: NiceChunk Roadbook | Roadmap & Future Plan
meta.description: NiceChunk Roadbook: a transparent future plan for a Solana-powered seeded voxel world, alpha gameplay, public rules, mining, forging, NCK utility, and future Guardian network.

hero.title: Roadbook for a player-owned voxel world.
hero.subtitle: NiceChunk is moving from long-form design into launch preparation: a seeded voxel world, public rules, browser gameplay, resource foundations, wallet identity, and a future Guardian network built around verifiable game state.
hero.body: This roadbook is written for players, builders, guilds, creators, and ecosystem partners who want a clear view of what is live, what is being prepared, and which risks are being handled openly.

stats.label: Design depth
stats.description: Long-form world, gameplay, resource, and economy design before public launch.

stats.label: Current stage
stats.description: Website, alpha client, public rules, resource atlas, and language systems are being prepared together.

stats.label: Planning horizon
stats.description: Covers genesis access, seasons, resource loops, Guardians, governance, and ecosystem expansion.

stats.label: Core principle
stats.description: Blockchain exists to support ownership, verification, and coordination, not to replace gameplay.

timeline.title: From alpha client to an open voxel economy.
timeline.body: Short-term milestones are written around concrete product work. Long-term milestones are intentionally framed as planned or exploratory, because world protocols must adapt to players, security reviews, chain constraints, and regulation.

phases.title: Foundation completed
phases.summary: NiceChunk has moved through a long design period covering world logic, player actions, resource foundations, economy direction, and public website structure.
goals: Define a game-first voxel world rather than a short-term token wrapper.
Build the foundation for seeded chunks, mining, forging, player identity, and resource rules.
Prepare a public surface where rules can be inspected before systems move on-chain.
deliverables: Seeded terrain engine and chunk generation demos.
Wallet identity and player setup flow.
Elements and block resource atlas direction.
Public fairness, documentation, and world-rule pages.

phases.title: Launch preparation
phases.summary: The current work is focused on trust before expansion: stable client loading, clear documentation, public rule pages, security review, and credible genesis messaging.
goals: Make the website understandable within seconds while preserving technical depth.
Turn world rules, resources, elements, and risk language into public pages.
Prepare alpha entry points for players without overclaiming decentralization.
deliverables: Roadbook page and transparent roadmap.
World rules, resource rules, elements, docs, and fairness pages.
Login and game loading progress improvements.
Chain storage design notes and security review preparation.

phases.title: Genesis alpha
phases.summary: Genesis should open the first real player loop: enter the world, explore chunks, mine blocks, forge tools, and prove that rules can survive user traffic.
goals: Bring early players into the browser world and collect real behavior data.
Validate mining, movement, inventory, and forging under alpha conditions.
Separate live features from planned chain features with clear wording.
deliverables: Genesis identity and early profile layer.
Season 0 alpha tasks and feedback loop.
Basic anti-bot and abuse monitoring.
Public changelog and issue-response process.

phases.title: Mining & forging loop
phases.summary: The project moves from a playable preview into a repeatable game loop with resources, tool durability, material logic, forging decisions, and player progression.
goals: Make block interaction meaningful through resources and crafting consequences.
Connect the 118-element foundation with block composition and material balance.
Create sinks, repairs, upgrades, and limits before expanding asset supply.
deliverables: Resource discovery and composition ranges.
Forging bench upgrades and tool rules.
Durability, repair, and material consumption loops.
Seasonal tuning reports for economy health.

phases.title: NCK economy & marketplace
phases.summary: NCK utility should support gameplay access, asset creation, settlement, and governance direction only when the game loops can justify economic weight.
goals: Use NCK around utility, settlement, participation, and future governance rather than price promises.
Move selected game state and economic actions toward verifiable chain records.
Design marketplace rules around player assets, fees, risk, and abuse prevention.
deliverables: Genesis access and NCK utility rules.
Marketplace settlement specification.
Asset metadata and chain-state boundaries.
Risk disclosure, permission policy, and monitoring procedures.

phases.title: Guardian network & governance
phases.summary: Guardians are planned as the access layer for live regions. They should help regions stay reachable and support communication without custodying assets or processing player transactions.
goals: Test region access nodes without giving them control over user assets.
Open more world parameters to transparent review and limited governance.
Support creators, guilds, APIs, events, and long-term community participation.
deliverables: Guardian node specification and region availability model.
Communication and region activity infrastructure.
Versioned world parameters and governance proposals.
Creator, guild, and ecosystem partnership programs.

current.title: Current stage: launch preparation.
current.body: The project has moved past abstract concept work. The current priority is trust-building before genesis: alpha client quality, public rule pages, security review, language infrastructure, resource definitions, and clear risk disclosure.
checklist: Make the public website explain NiceChunk within seconds.
Keep alpha gameplay, wallet identity, and loading flows stable.
Publish world generation, resource, element, and fairness pages.
Document chunk storage direction and PDA compression tradeoffs.
Prepare smart contract tests, permission boundaries, and review process.
Keep risk language explicit: no profit promise, no buyback promise, no fixed rewards.

future.title: What makes the world worth returning to.

items.title: Seasonal world content
items.body: New biomes, resource events, forged item goals, exploration milestones, and world-rule updates that keep players returning for gameplay.

items.title: Healthy asset loops
items.body: Controlled output, meaningful consumption, tool durability, repair, upgrades, forging decisions, and resource sinks before large-scale asset expansion.

items.title: Guild and social layer
items.body: Region activity, group objectives, shared exploration, leaderboards, communication, and future Guardian-supported local coordination.

items.title: Creator participation
items.body: Guides, streams, videos, community tools, resource research, map discoveries, and public explanations that make the world easier to enter.

items.title: Transparent operations
items.body: Public rules, versioned updates, incident notes, security reviews, anti-bot policy, and monthly progress communication.

items.title: Open world protocol
items.body: APIs, verifiable state, governance direction, Guardian network, and long-term tooling for a player-owned voxel world.

trust.title: Confidence comes from visible rules, not slogans.
trust.body: NiceChunk should be judged by what players can inspect: world generation, resource foundations, wallet boundaries, action rules, chain storage direction, and the limits of what is live today.

items.title: Public world rules
items.body: Terrain generation, chunk previews, biome logic, water, mountains, resource signals, and block rules are published for inspection.

items.title: Verifiable seeds
items.body: Chunks are generated from seeds and coordinates so the same public inputs can rebuild the same world under a versioned rule set.

items.title: Wallet boundaries
items.body: Players interact with Solana wallets directly. The project must clearly separate signed player intent from client-only preview logic.

items.title: No-custody Guardians
items.body: Guardians are planned to help regions stay reachable and communicate; they should not custody assets or process player transactions.

items.title: Risk disclosure
items.body: The project avoids guaranteed rewards, buyback claims, price promises, and investment framing. Participation carries risk.

items.title: Versioned updates
items.body: Language files, world configs, public docs, and future protocol rules should be versioned so players can see when assumptions change.

confidence.title: Near-term work is more certain. Long-term systems stay adaptive.

items.reason: Core design, website direction, alpha client, and public rule surfaces already exist.
Current work is concrete: pages, client quality, language infrastructure, and security preparation.
Depends on test feedback, wallet flow reliability, early players, and abuse controls.
Depends on resource balance, material tuning, durability loops, and player retention.
Depends on security review, chain constraints, asset rules, compliance, and real demand.
Long-term systems depend on node incentives, region activity, infrastructure, and governance maturity.

security.title: Game-first, transparent, and careful about promises.
security.body: Web3 games earn trust by shipping playable systems, publishing rules, protecting players, and avoiding financial promises. NiceChunk treats digital assets as game-state and identity extensions, not guaranteed-return products.
items: Smart contract tests and security review preparation.
Clear boundaries for wallet signatures, client state, and chain state.
Key permissions designed for review, monitoring, and future multisig control.
Anti-bot, abnormal minting, transfer, and market behavior monitoring.
User agreement, privacy policy, and risk disclosure before broader launch.
No profit promise, no buyback promise, no fixed reward promise.
security.notice: Digital assets may fluctuate in value. NiceChunk does not promise profit, buybacks, fixed rewards, or asset appreciation. Users should evaluate participation risk according to their own jurisdiction.

risks.title: Risks are clearer when they are written down.

items.impact: Players may leave after first exploration.
items.response: Test core movement, mining, and forging before expanding asset issuance.

items.impact: Materials may lose meaning if output grows too fast.
items.response: Use scarcity, durability, repair, forging costs, and seasonal tuning.

items.impact: Rewards and data may be distorted.
items.response: Use behavior limits, wallet reputation, rate controls, and manual review.

items.impact: Assets and trust can be damaged.
items.response: Run tests, audits, monitoring, emergency controls, and limited rollout.

items.impact: Certain features or regions may require adjustment.
items.response: Avoid investment language, publish risk notices, and keep features modular.

items.impact: External cycles can affect attention and funding.
items.response: Reduce speculation dependence and focus on real players and content.

cta.title: Help shape the first playable era of NiceChunk.
cta.body: Early players, builders, creators, guilds, and infrastructure partners can help test the world loop, improve rules, and make the public protocol easier to understand.
